steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content:
Divide by $8$, then square.
$$\sqrt{x}=6\;\Rightarrow\;x=36$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice C is correct. It's given that $f\left(x\right)=8\sqrt{x}$. Substituting $48$ for $f\left(x\right)$ in this equation yields $48=8\sqrt{x}$. Dividing both sides of this equation by $8$ yields $6=\sqrt{x}$. This can be rewritten as $\sqrt{x}=6$. Squaring both sides of this equation yields $x=36$. Therefore, the value of $x$ for which $f\left(x\right)=48$ is $36$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is incorrect. If $x=6$, $f\left(x\right)=8\sqrt{6}$, not $48$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B is incorrect. If $x=8$, $f\left(x\right)=8\sqrt{8}$, not $48$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice D is incorrect. If $x=64$, $f\left(x\right)=8\sqrt{64}$, which is equivalent to $64$, not $48$.
